steps.action: In a wider sense, the term is used to refer to a with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: In a wider sense, the term is used to refer to a with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: in continuous evolution together with rhythm, th
steps.text: in continuous evolution together with rhythm, the poetic stanzas, and the ambiance.

steps.action: These Roma migrants also brought bells, and an e
steps.text: These Roma migrants also brought bells, and an extensive repertoire of songs and dances.

steps.action: This centuries-long period of cultural interming
steps.text: This centuries-long period of cultural intermingling, formed the roots of flamenco song and dance genres.

steps.action: Locals learned the cantes, while reinterpreting
steps.text: Locals learned the cantes, while reinterpreting the Andalusian folk songs in their own style, expanding the repertoire.

steps.action: In the line of purism, the poet Federico García
steps.text: In the line of purism, the poet Federico García Lorca and the composer Manuel de Falla had the idea of concurso de cante jondo in Granada in 1922.

steps.action: The jury was chaired by Antonio Chacón, who at t
steps.text: The jury was chaired by Antonio Chacón, who at that time was the leading figure in cante.

steps.action: At that time, there were already flamenco record
steps.text: At that time, there were already flamenco recordings related to Christmas, which can be divided into two groups: the traditional flamenco carol and flamenco songs that adapt their lyrics to the Christmas theme.

steps.action: It was the Golden Age of this genre, with figure
steps.text: It was the Golden Age of this genre, with figures such as Antonio Chacón, Manuel Vallejo, Manuel Torre, La Niña de los Peines, Pepe Marchena and Manolo Caracol.

steps.action: , on flamenco singing and dancing.
steps.text: , on flamenco singing and dancing.

steps.action: They also maintained that the Andalusian gitanos
steps.text: They also maintained that the Andalusian gitanos had contributed decisively to their formation, highlighting the exceptional nature of flamenco among gypsy music and dances from other parts of Spain and Europe.

steps.action: "Pienso en tu mirá", "Di mi nombre" or the song
steps.text: "Pienso en tu mirá", "Di mi nombre" or the song that catapulted her to fame, "Malamente", are a combination of styles that includes a flamenco/south Spain traditional musical base.

steps.action: Rosalía has broken the limits of this musical ge
steps.text: Rosalía has broken the limits of this musical genre by embracing other urban rhythms, but has also created a lot of controversy about which genre is she using.

steps.action: Flamenco is closely associated to the gitanos of
steps.text: Flamenco is closely associated to the gitanos of the Romani ethnicity who have contributed significantly to its origination and professionalization.

steps.action: However, its style is uniquely Andalusian and fl
steps.text: However, its style is uniquely Andalusian and flamenco artists have historically included Spaniards of both gitano and non-gitano heritage.

steps.action: The oldest record of flamenco music dates to 177
steps.text: The oldest record of flamenco music dates to 1774 in the book Las Cartas Marruecas (The Moroccan Letters) by José Cadalso.

steps.action: " On 16 November 2010, UNESCO declared flamenco
steps.text: " On 16 November 2010, UNESCO declared flamenco one of the Masterpieces of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity.

steps.action: According to flamencologist Cristina Cruces-Rold
steps.text: According to flamencologist Cristina Cruces-Roldán, a year prior to Borrow's account, there already existed a gitano party in Madrid that was clearly identified as flamenco.

steps.action: The first use of the term flamenco to refer to t
steps.text: The first use of the term flamenco to refer to the music genre appears in a 1847 newspaper article of El Espectador where it was referred to as a "gitano genre".

steps.action: In the early years of flamenco, the term was ver
steps.text: In the early years of flamenco, the term was versatile and was used to refer to a variety of concepts in the gitano-Andalusian world.

steps.action: For example, in the 1860s-70s this versatility w
steps.text: For example, in the 1860s-70s this versatility was exemplified through its use to refer to a musical style and a certain aesthetic, manners, and way of life that were perceived to be gitano.

steps.action: At that time, flamenco was not a strict genre bu
steps.text: At that time, flamenco was not a strict genre but a way of performing music in a gitano-Andalusian style.

steps.action: The North Indian scales of flamenco were introdu
steps.text: The North Indian scales of flamenco were introduced to Andalusia by the Romani migrations from Northern India.

steps.action: Some of the instruments associated with flamenco
steps.text: Some of the instruments associated with flamenco and Spanish folklore in different regions today, are the wooden castanets and tambourines, both believed to originate during the Al Andalus period.
